title: knautia pannonica
intro: Knautia pannonica, also known as Hungarian pellitory, is a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its pink or white flowers and edible roots. The medicinal properties of Knautia pannonica include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Knautia pannonica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
